commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the rich Overview provider row with a compact LiteSummary row and adds focused tests for the new summary model.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this review. The related issue provides macOS 27 sample evidence and current source maps to the hosted SwiftUI Overview row path, but this PR lacks an after-fix scroll profile or recording.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This draft needs maintainer direction, real scroll proof, and a live-model fix before it can be considered for merge.

target.title: Lighten Overview menu rows
target.body:
Refs #1674.

## Summary
- Replace the full rich Overview provider row with a compact `LiteSummary` row.
- Avoid rendering `InlineUsageDashboardContent` / chart-heavy usage sections in the Overview list.
- Keep provider click behavior, provider detail submenus, live refresh subtitles, account/storage text, and status styling intact.

## Why this direction
The scroll sample in #1674 points at repeated SwiftUI/AppKit view layout work during every menu scroll event. This version reduces the amount of SwiftUI content each Overview row owns, so the menu has less to relayout while scrolling. It is the lower-risk performance direction if maintainers are comfortable with a denser/lighter Overview presentation.

This is an alternative to the rich-row PR; they are not meant to merge together.

## Tests
- `swift test --filter OverviewMenuCardRowViewTests`
- `swift test --filter "overview row"`
- `swift build`
- `make check`
- `git diff --check`

## Not covered
- I do not have a fresh macOS 27 live scroll profile after this patch. The branch is intended to give maintainers a concrete, test-covered option to validate against the lag in #1674.
target.author: hhh2210
